items.title: Loss Functions (MSE / MAE)
items.description: MSE and MAE share the same core formulas as RMSE and MAE but serve as training losses optimized during fit. Loss Functions is the canonical home for per-example (y − ŷ)² and |y − ŷ| as objectives — link there; this topic covers held-out evaluation, interpretation, and reporting.

items.title: Expectation & Variance
items.description: MSE on residuals estimates E[e²]; MAE estimates E[|e|]. When mean residual ≈ 0, MSE relates to Var(e). RMSE parallels standard deviation σ = √Var(X) in sharing units with the quantity measured.

items.title: Linear Regression
items.description: OLS linear regression is typically trained with MSE and judged on test RMSE or MAE. This topic supplies the metric formulas; Linear Regression covers fitting ŷ = Xw + b and the normal equation.

items.title: Train/Val/Test Splits
items.description: RMSE and MAE must be computed on held-out validation or test data — not training data used for fitting. Train/Val/Test Splits defines split discipline and leakage avoidance for trustworthy metric values.
